title: Forecast of Annual Average Water Controlled by Reservoir Dams and Estimated Public-Sector Water Supply (Agriculture / Cities & Industries), by Regional Zone -- Fourth and Fifth Development Plans
source_org: Statistical Centre of Iran (1972 Statistical Yearbook), cited by World Bank appraisal mission
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION with visual verification. Source: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1975_water_supply_sewerage_vol2_annexes.pdf, Volume II Annex 4 Table 4.3, PDF page 33. Rendered to PNG at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -png -r 300 -f 33 -l 33` and read directly with the Read tool. Several source cells print as the OCR-ambiguous string '000' where the table clearly intends a genuine zero value in context (row/column totals reconcile correctly when treated as 0) -- these are transcribed as 0 in this CSV; this is a disclosed interpretive judgment, not a source-copy error, and is flagged here rather than silently applied.
time_coverage: Fourth Development Plan (1968-1972) actual/end-point + Fifth Development Plan (1973-1977) forecast
frequency: single cross-section by regional zone (not an annual time series)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document; underlying data originally from Iran's 1972 Statistical Yearbook (Statistical Centre of Iran)
notes: Regional breakdown showing Khuzestan (site of the Dez and Karun dams) as by far the largest water-control zone (15,112 Mm3 by end of 5th Plan, ~57% of the national 26,280 Mm3 total) -- direct quantitative confirmation of Khuzestan's centrality to Iran's Pahlavi-era dam-building program, consistent with the Dez Multipurpose Project data (wb1960dez-* datasets) and the major-dams table (wb1975water-major-dams-specifications-1971) in this same round. Companion datasets: wb1975water-major-dams-specifications-1971 (Table 4.1) and wb1975water-diversion-dams-specifications (Table 4.2). Part of this project's Round 38 (mining/dams/industrial subsectors) hunt.
